description: Tier-A automatable eval cases (deterministic, no Aseprite, no LLM). Each maps to the skill/agent/hook it grades, for coverage reporting. Tier-B LLM-judge cases are run manually (see evals/README.md).

cases.asserts: goblin-default skin ramp is hue-shifted (hue spread across ramp) and value-monotonic, not value-only
batch drawing tools are blocked; live_* and export are allowed
a clean on-palette sprite produces zero linter findings
the golden sprite diffs to zero against itself
a changed sprite diffs non-zero against the golden
a clean walk strip keeps adjacent-frame silhouette IoU above the 0.80 drift floor
a deliberately-ballooned frame drops silhouette IoU below the floor (cross-frame proportion drift caught)
the SessionStart hook emits valid additionalContext JSON
every Tier-B LLM-judge case (evals/tier_b.json) is well-formed: maps to a real component + checklist id, cites existing rule files, and rubric weights sum to 1.0
the long-session degradation helper (judge.compute_slope) flags a decaying quality series and passes a stable one
the project's own ramps lint as good (value-monotonic, hue-shifted, mid-peaked saturation) and a value-only grey ramp is flagged
the native-grid auto-detect recovers a 4x-upscale's cell size (de-fake) and leaves native art at cell 1
a well-timed clip (in-band idle; attack with a >=150ms impact hold and easing) yields zero error-level timing findings against knowledge/timing-budgets.json
a jittery (too-fast) idle, a held-less uniform attack, and an infinitely looping death are all flagged — the rules/04 timing doctrine as a deterministic gate

timing_lint ingests the raw live data contract — live_list_frames duration in SECONDS + 1-based frameNumber, live_list_tags fromFrame/toFrame/repeats — converting correctly (0.12s -> 120ms flagged, 0.2s passes)
lint_sprite derives a size-appropriate colour budget from knowledge/scale-hierarchy.json (tighter caps for smaller sprites) and flags an over-cap sprite
the pre-export guard passes a clean integer scale and rejects a fractional or nonpositive export scale (pixel-grid destruction)
